paragraphs: The Native Thyme sold under the Tucker Bush brand is Prostanthera incisa, the cut-leaf mint-bush, a Lamiaceae (mint family) shrub endemic to south-eastern Australia from the NSW coast to Victoria. Despite the common name it is not a true thyme; it is an erect, openly branched shrub roughly 0.5 to 3 metres tall with hairy, glandular, coarsely toothed leaves and pale mauve flowers in spring.

The aromatic, glandular leaves carry a complex savoury flavour with herbal, peppery and citrus tones, used as a native seasoning herb. "Tucker Bush" is the WA native-bushfood brand the plant is sold under.

claims.text: Tucker Bush Native Thyme is Prostanthera incisa, the cut-leaf mint-bush, family Lamiaceae, endemic to south-eastern Australia
Erect openly branched shrub about 0.5 to 3 metres tall, hairy glandular toothed leaves, pale mauve flowers
Aromatic leaves used as a native seasoning herb with herbal, peppery and citrus notes

sources.name: Native Thyme, Prostanthera incisa, Tucker Bush
